masthead.title: Everything I Make
masthead.dek: The full shelf from the desk that wrote THE SIGNAL CHAIN — the books, the essays, the apps, and the company underneath them
masthead.standfirst: One catalog for the whole body of work — Jason Colapietro, who plays as Johnny Suede. It starts where this site starts: guitar and tone, a book about six strings and the life around them. From there it widens to three more books on AI and who owns what a creator makes, the apps I build for musicians, and Suede Labs AI, the company that puts ownership back in the artist's hands. Pick a shelf and start reading.
masthead.seo_description: The full catalog from Jason Colapietro (Johnny Suede): THE SIGNAL CHAIN, the Print the Quiet essays, the Suede 100, three books on AI and ownership, the apps, and Suede Labs AI.

sections.title: Read — guitar & tone
sections.intro: This is the writing the whole site is built around — guitar, tone, and the parts of a record that never make it onto a lead sheet.

items.name: The Signal Chain
items.blurb: A guitarist's history of amps, effects, and electric tone, braided with memoir, theory, a songbook, and 50 song lessons. Chapter one free.

items.name: Print the Quiet
items.blurb: Nine free essays on tone and dynamics — Jeff Buckley's Hallelujah, the loudness war, the compressor, the sound of the room itself.

items.name: The Suede 100
items.blurb: Every effects pedal worth naming, ranked 1 to 100, head to head. No honorable mentions, no genre excuses. The list the press won't print.

sections.title: More books — AI, ownership & the creative future
sections.intro: Same hand, different string. These titles trade the fretboard for the bigger question: who owns creative work once the machines can make it too.

items.name: Suede Labs: The Human Authenticity Layer
items.blurb: The case that origin and ownership are the only marks AI can't fake — and the layer Suede Labs builds to prove them.

items.name: Proof as Infrastructure
items.blurb: How to build systems that hold up when nobody trusts anybody — proof baked in, not bolted on. The blueprint under the registry.

items.name: Stake Your Claim
items.blurb: Speeches and hard truths on flipping the AI onslaught into an asset you own — autonomous agents, real claims, generational wealth.

sections.title: Play & practice — the apps
sections.intro: Five tools built by a guitarist who got tired of practice apps that nag. A coach that answers in plain English, three studios that take your craft seriously, and a companion that pushes back.

items.name: Strumly
items.blurb: Ask your guitar coach anything, any hour, and get a straight answer — tuner, metronome, chord and scale tools, song help, and an ear trainer built in

items.name: Suede Studio Guitar
items.blurb: A real guitar studio on your iPhone: tuner, timing drills, chord tools, a rig workbench, setup diagnostics, room health, and session capture

items.name: Suede Studio Voice
items.blurb: Map your range, watch your register shifts, and build voice routines that hold up. Local-first — your practice stays on your phone

items.name: Suede Studio Muse
items.blurb: Think out loud with a dry-wit bandmate who works ideas with you and tells you when they're bad. Not a yes-man

items.name: Suede Studio Inspiration
items.blurb: A workspace for AI-assisted musical ideas that keeps the rights yours: provenance notes, ownership context, and signed creative records

sections.title: The platform — Suede Labs AI
sections.intro: Suede Labs AI is the substrate under everything else: tooling that lets a musician prove they made a thing, define who can use it, and get paid when it gets used.

items.name: Suede Labs AI
items.blurb: The company under it all. Proof of creation, programmable rights, and royalty settlement — so artists hold the title to their work as AI eats the room.

items.name: The Suede IP Registry
items.blurb: Register a work, prove you made it first, and set the terms anyone licenses it on — ownership and provenance, on the public record.
